epic_title: Signal, Analyst Workbench and Internal Draft Governance

baseline.commit_message: docs: add epic 09 domain gap report

ticket_definition_reread.task: Define target surfaces, state machine, role guards, payload visibility, audit/evidence requirements and acceptance criteria for Signal, Analyst Workbench and Internal Draft Governance.
ticket_definition_reread.goal: Make implementation decision-complete for this domain.
definition_of_done: Acceptance criteria cover positive and negative paths.
UI, service and test boundaries are named.

contract_decision.summary: EPIC-09 implementation must promote S033/S034/S035 into a typed process-first AREA-04 contract before UI implementation claims can be accepted.
contract_decision.first_implementation_rule: No new route is required for the first EPIC-09 implementation slice; any route, API, schema, advisor/compliance boundary or client-visible payload expansion is a stop-and-report decision.

target_surfaces.primary_job: Orient the internal signal and draft area around one next analyst action without duplicating trigger detail.
target_surfaces.viewport_rule: Area status, blocker and next action must be visible without scrolling in the target viewport.

target_surfaces.primary_job: Select one analyst work item, expose current signal/draft state, blocker and allowed handoff without client visibility.
target_surfaces.viewport_rule: Queue, selected object, blocker and one primary next action must fit above-fold; secondary metrics move to compact proof/drawer context.

target_surfaces.primary_job: Review one trigger/internal draft object, resolve evidence or unsupported-claim blockers and route only safe advisor candidates.
target_surfaces.viewport_rule: Trigger identity, draft state, unsupported-claim/evidence blocker and primary recovery action must be visible without scrolling; audit/history/details are secondary.

state_machine.meaning: A signal exists, is internal and has not yet produced client-visible advice.
Analyst workbench selected one item and is deciding internal route or recovery.
A signal or draft cannot proceed until evidence or data-quality recovery is created.
An AI/rules draft exists only as internal preparation.
The draft needs classification and internal safety review.
One or more draft claims lack evidence or policy support.
Analyst rejected a draft internally with reason and audit trail.
Rebuild is blocked until accepted scoped evidence exists.
Internal draft was rebuilt from accepted scoped evidence and remains internal.
Internal-only fields and rationale are removed from any downstream client/export candidate.
A safe internal candidate can be handed to advisor review with evidence/source trace; advisor and compliance gates remain separate.

role_guards.hard_negative: Signal intake creates no autonomous advice, no release, no export approval and no client visibility.
Wrong role or wrong tenant gets deny/blocked/hidden without payload leak or mutation.
Action item creation cannot make a recommendation, trigger or evidence item client-visible.
Evidence-gap identification cannot mark sufficiency or route unsupported draft content to advisor.
Draft classification or rejection cannot release, export, approve or expose internal draft payload to clients.
Rebuild without accepted scoped evidence is blocked and audited; admin/advisor/compliance cannot force analyst rebuild by role override.
Route-to-advisor may set advisor-pending only; it cannot set advisor-approved, compliance-released, export-ready or clientVisible.
Client roles, export projection and client-safe APIs receive only released/redacted summaries and no internal draft, notes, trace internals or raw claims.

acceptance_criteria_by_process.positive: A valid signal creates an internal trigger, queue item and audit event with no autonomous advice.
acceptance_criteria_by_process.negative: Unsupported source, confidence, severity, role or tenant scope is rejected without mutation or payload leak.

acceptance_criteria_by_process.positive: Workbench triage moves one selected signal through assign, block, request-evidence or advisor-handoff states with audit.
acceptance_criteria_by_process.negative: Triage cannot approve advice, release content, export content or create client visibility.

acceptance_criteria_by_process.positive: Action item creation records owner, reason, source trigger and internal-only visibility.
acceptance_criteria_by_process.negative: Action creation cannot create client-visible recommendation, evidence sufficiency or downstream release.

acceptance_criteria_by_process.positive: Evidence gap identification either creates a scoped evidence request or blocks the signal/draft with a recoverable reason.
acceptance_criteria_by_process.negative: Evidence gap state cannot be treated as sufficiency, advisor readiness or client-safe advice.

acceptance_criteria_by_process.positive: Data-quality issue routing creates an internal work item with owner, priority, blocker and recovery path.
acceptance_criteria_by_process.negative: Data-quality routing cannot silently skip evidence, advisor or compliance gates.

acceptance_criteria_by_process.positive: Internal AI/rules draft generation creates an internal draft state with source context and explicit no-client boundary.
acceptance_criteria_by_process.negative: Generated draft cannot appear in client, export or client-safe API payloads.

acceptance_criteria_by_process.positive: Draft classification marks draft type, review state, forbidden-payload boundary and next safe action.
acceptance_criteria_by_process.negative: Classification cannot mark content client-safe, advisor-approved or compliance-released.

acceptance_criteria_by_process.positive: Unsupported claims are surfaced as blockers with request-evidence, reject or rebuild recovery path.
acceptance_criteria_by_process.negative: Unsupported claims cannot be routed to advisor or export/client projections.

acceptance_criteria_by_process.positive: Draft rejection records reason, actor, audit event and internal-only recovery state.
acceptance_criteria_by_process.negative: Draft rejection cannot imply client rejection, advisor rejection, compliance block or export failure.

acceptance_criteria_by_process.positive: Rebuild succeeds only when scoped evidence is accepted and source trace is preserved.
acceptance_criteria_by_process.negative: Rebuild without evidence fails closed and leaves advisor/compliance/client/export states unchanged.

acceptance_criteria_by_process.positive: Internal-only redaction removes forbidden payloads from downstream candidates while preserving internal proof.
acceptance_criteria_by_process.negative: Redaction cannot be treated as compliance release, export approval or client acceptance.

acceptance_criteria_by_process.positive: Source/evidence traceability ties a draft candidate to safe evidence/source references before advisor handoff.
acceptance_criteria_by_process.negative: Missing source trace blocks handoff and never exposes raw trace internals to clients.

screenshot_proof.viewport_rule: The primary job, selected object, blocker and one next action should fit without scrolling in the tested viewport; any exception must be explicitly approved and documented.

decision_gate.next_recommended_action: Re-read EPIC-09-IMPL-01, verify dependency commits in the current baseline, then start EPIC-09-IMPL-01A only if satisfied.

screenshot_decision.reason: Specification-only ticket; no visible UI changed.
